items.title: npm run dev orchestrates api + worker + web in one terminal
steps: 1. Run concurrently with the same -n api,worker,web -c blue,yellow,green flags over three commands
2. Observe one labeled, colored stream per command
items.expected: Three labeled streams [api] [worker] [web] start and report independently
items.evidence: npx concurrently -n api,worker,web -c blue,yellow,green "echo API-OK" "echo WORKER-OK" "echo WEB-OK" -> printed [api] API-OK / [worker] WORKER-OK / [web] WEB-OK
items.notes: Stand-in echo commands prove the -n/-c flags and three-stream spawning; the real dev script swaps in npm:dev:api/worker/web.

items.title: Backend-only startup preserved as npm run dev:api; all targets resolve
steps: 2. Confirm dev, dev:api, dev:worker, dev:web exist
3. Confirm src/index.ts, src/workers/worker.ts, and frontend dev script exist
items.expected: dev:api -> tsx watch src/index.ts (unchanged old behavior); every referenced script and entry file resolves
items.evidence: Script check: OK dev / dev:api -> tsx watch src/index.ts / dev:worker / dev:web. Files: OK src/index.ts, OK src/workers/worker.ts, OK frontend dev -> vite.

items.title: concurrently present in devDependencies
items.notes: Same dependency the sibling repos (alloro-chat, alloro-os) use.

items.title: Full live end-to-end boot of the three real long-running processes
items.precondition: .env pointing at reachable dev DB + Redis
steps: 1. From repo root: npm run dev
3. Ctrl-C once to stop all three
items.expected: Backend listening, worker connected to Redis, Vite ready — all under one process
items.notes: Owner can run npm run dev to watch all three live.
items.waiver: Deliberately not auto-run: booting the dev worker makes BullMQ immediately pull live dev-queue jobs (rankings/PMS/summary, some client-email-producing). Orchestration wiring is environment-independent and fully proven by T1-T3; a live boot is a safe owner action, not a correctness gate for this change.
